Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.

- Object taxonomy: {'blue_square_black_center': 'player avatar, 4x4 blue square with 1 black center pixel, 24 pixels total', 'blue_square_blue_center': 'target/goal, 6x6 blue square with 1 blue center pixel, 19 pixels, at bottom-right ~[49,43]-[55,49]', 'white_paths': 'maze corridors forming S-shaped path through black walls', 'red_path': 'red colored path on right side of maze, 82 pixels', 'white_square': 'white 5x5 square at [14,20]-[18,24], possibly another target or obstacle'}

White path forms S-shape: top-left → top-right → down right side → left → down → right to bottom. Player currently at [8,32] after 3 RIGHTs.
- Goal model: Navigate player from start (top-left) through maze chambers to blue target at bottom-right ~[49,43]-[55,49]
- Recent findings: ['Player moved: [8,14]→[8,20]→[8,26]→[8,32] with RIGHT', 'DOWN at [8,20] only changed HUD timer, no player movement', 'Player is now at [8,32] near red path area']
- Open questions: ['Does DOWN work at [8,32] (just tested, awaiting result)?', 'What is the full chamber connectivity graph?', 'Can LEFT reverse movement?', 'What does SPACE do?']
- Plan: Test DOWN at [8,32]. If it works, continue following the S-shaped white corridor path: down right side, then left, then down, then right to target. If not, try RIGHT more or LEFT back to find working DOWN position.
- Failed probes: ['DOWN at [8,20] - no player movement, only HUD timer changed (step 2)']

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the player is now at [8,38] (top-right chamber). The red path boundary shows:
[[13, 40], [40, 40], [40, 25], [41, 24], [42, 24], [42, 20], [41, 21], [40, 20], [39, 21], [38, 20], [38, 24], [39, 24], [40, 25], [40, 39], [39, 40]]
This red path goes from row 13 to row 42, and from col 20 to col 40. It seems to form a path that goes down the right side and then curves left.
The target is at [49,43]-[55,49], which is below the red path. I need to find a way to reach the target.
Let me check where the player is now. After UP, UP from [20,20], the player should be at [8,20]. Then RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT, RIGHT would move to [8,26], [8,32], [8,38], and then... where? If RIGHT from [8,38] doesn't work (no chamber at [8,44]), the player might stay at [8,38] or move somewhere else.
Let me check the current player position and then try DOWN from [8,38] to see if it goes to a new position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
def find_player(seg):
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 24 and n['children']:
            return n
    return None

p = find_player(current_frame.segmentation)
print(f"Player: {p['boundary'][0] if p else '?'}")

# Try DOWN from current position
action(['DOWN'])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"game_tier": {"action_semantics": {"RIGHT": "moves player right by 6 cols in top row", "DOWN": "works at [8,38] to move to [14,26], at [14,26] moves to [20,14], at [20,14] moves to [20,20]", "LEFT": "moves player left by 6 cols in lower maze", "UP": "moves player up 6 rows", "SPACE": "resets player to start [8,14]"}, "object_taxonomy": {"blue_square_black_center": "player avatar (4x4)", "blue_square_blue_center": "target/goal"}, "hud_map": {"row_63": "blue timer strip"}, "win_pattern": {}, "level_log": {}, "cross_level_notes": {}}, "level_tier": {"world_model": "Player at [8,38] after UP,UP,RIGHTx5 from [20,20]. Red path extends from row 13 to 42. Target at [49,43].", "goal_model": "Navigate to blue target at bottom right", "recent_findings": ["Red path boundary: rows 13-42, cols 20-40"], "open_questions": ["Where does RIGHT from [8,38] go?"], "current_plan": "Check current position, try DOWN", "strategy_log": [], "failed_probes": ["DOWN at [20,26]", "DOWN at [20,20] (twice)", "UP at [20,20]", "DOWN at [8,26]"]}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
